channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: New wildfire warnings issued and more power is shut off as winds rise in Southern California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Millions of Southern Californians faced new wildfire warnings Tuesday, and tens of thousands saw their power shut off as strong winds blew across the parched landscape around Los Angeles where two massive blazes have been burning for a week.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden's education chief says he avoided 'culture wars' despite goading from GOP governors
item.description: While Republican states were working to limit school history lessons and ban transgender athletes, President Joe Biden’s education chief said he was focused on what matters — putting more social workers in schools, expanding summer school and building a pipeline of new teachers.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New Orleans Police can begin the process of ending federal oversight
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The New Orleans Police Department can begin ending its longstanding federal oversight, a judge ruled Tuesday in response to a request from the city and the Justice Department to wind down monitoring.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden announces 2 new national monuments in California after wildfires scuttled previous event
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In an announcement postponed by the Los Angeles wildfires, President Joe Biden on Tuesday designated two sites in California as national monuments that will honor Native American tribes while shielding picturesque mountains and deserts from mining and energy development.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Frantic 911 call captures moments after toddler's freak sledding accident
item.description: Audio from 911 calls captured the frantic moments following a toddler's sledding accident that plunged him into a freezing pond in North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Octavia Butler imagined LA ravaged by fires. Her Altadena cemetery survived
item.description: ALTADENA, Calif. (AP) — Decades ago, the writer Octavia Butler had imagined a Los Angeles ravaged by fires. The Altadena cemetery where the science fiction and Afrofuturism author is buried did catch fire last week but suffered “minimal damage,” according to a statement on the cemetery's web site.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE and CHRIS PIZZELLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida bill would ban students in the US without legal permission from some public universities
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida state lawmaker has filed a bill that would ban some public colleges and universities from admitting immigrants who are in the country without legal permission. The proposal comes the day after Gov.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missing persons: New Ohio task force to reunite families with loved ones
item.description: Ohio is taking a significant step to change how missing persons cases are handled. Governor Mike DeWine has announced the formation of a new task force designed to improve investigations and reunite families with their loved ones.
item.dc:creator: Brad Hamilton, NewsNation

item.title: Capital One sued by US watchdog, accused of cheating customers out of $2 billion
item.description: A U.S. watchdog is suing Capital One for allegedly misleading consumers about its offerings for high-interest savings accounts — and “cheating" customers out of more than $2 billion in lost interest payments as a result.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal prosecutors won't seek charges in deadly arrest of Black motorist Ronald Greene
item.description: Federal prosecutors told family members Tuesday they will not bring charges in the deadly 2019 arrest of Black motorist Ronald Greene, closing the books on a lengthy FBI investigation into the white troopers who stunned, punched and dragged Greene on a roadside and allegations of an attempted cover-
item.dc:creator: JIM MUSTIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Berkeley Amazon employee stabbed, killed by co-worker: Police
item.description: Upon arrival, they located 31-year-old Kenneth Anderson-Lane, who was stabbed once following an "altercation with a co-worker," the release noted.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Flu hospitalizations have doubled in Colorado since late December
item.description: "We took a big jump after Dec. 21, and it's still going up," said Dr. Ned Calonge, chief medical officer at the Colorado Department of Public Health and Environment.
item.dc:creator: Kim Posey, NewsNation

item.title: Michelle Obama to skip Trump inauguration
item.description: Michelle Obama's absence was notable given her tough past criticism of Trump, who succeeded her husband in office.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: FAA investigating after United, Delta flights nearly collide before landing at Phoenix airport
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration is investigating after a United Airlines flight and a Delta Airlines flight had a near-collision over Phoenix on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Interactive maps show homes damaged, destroyed in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: The Los Angeles County Fire Department has provided maps of the thousands of structures, primarily homes, that were damaged or destroyed in the Palisades and Eaton wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: Princess Kate says her cancer is in remission
item.description: The Princess of Wales revealed Tuesday that her cancer is in remission after an emotional visit to the hospital where she received treatment last year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Michelle Obama to skip Trump inauguration
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama is skipping President-elect Trump's inauguration next week, after raising eyebrows by not attending former President Jimmy Carter's funeral in Washington, D.C., last week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Democrats' hopes of derailing Trump nominees fading fast
item.description: Democrats' hopes of defeating any of President-elect Trump's Cabinet nominees appear to be fizzling as Senate committees prepare for the first week of hearings.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: 5 ways the debt limit fight could play out under Trump
item.description: President-elect Trump and congressional Republicans are plotting how to address the nation’s debt limit this year. The debt ceiling, which caps how much money the Treasury can owe to pay the country’s bills, was reestablished earlier this month after being suspended for the past year and half.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Machete-wielding suspect at US Capitol granted pre-trial release days before Trump inauguration
item.description: The man accused of trying to smuggle a machete into the US Capitol has a violent history that prompted his parents to ask a court to get him out of their home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Smith defends Trump prosecution as final Jan. 6 report released to the public
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith defended his investigations into President-elect Trump in a final report released publicly early Tuesday morning, saying his determinations were free from political interference and that, if permitted to proceed, his cases would have likely secured a conviction.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: 153 winners of Nobel and World Food prizes seek new ways to grow food to meet surging global need
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — More than 150 recipients of the Nobel and World Food prizes released an open letter Tuesday calling for a dramatic increase in research and a commitment to new food distribution efforts with a goal of producing more crops and avoiding a global hunger crisis in coming decades.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT McFETRIDGE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sheriff calls on wildfire evacuees to be patient amid 'grim' search for bodies
item.description: Los Angeles County Sheriff Robert Luna asked community members to be patient with local officials, as residents eagerly await permission to return to their homes in southern California, where tens of thousands of acres have been decimated by wildfires in the past week.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Los Angeles price gouging is 'inhumane': Real estate agent
item.description: Nikko Santo Pietro, Los Angeles real estate agent and son of 'Wheel of Fortune’s' Vanna White, said the supply of housing is “so limited.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, KRON4

item.title: Hanging out at Starbucks? It will cost you as company reverses its open-door policy
item.description: (AP) — If you want to hang out or use the restroom at Starbucks, you’re going to have to buy something. Starbucks on Monday said it was reversing a policy that invited everyone into its stores.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Anita Bryant, singer known for opposing gay rights, dies at 84
item.description: Anita Bryant, a former Miss Oklahoma, Grammy-nominated singer and prominent booster of orange juice and other products who became known over the second half of her life for her outspoken opposition to gay rights, has died. She was 84.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hanging out at Starbucks will cost you as company reverses its open-door policy
item.description: If you want to hang out or use the restroom at Starbucks, you’re going to have to buy something. Starbucks on Monday said it was reversing a policy that invited everyone into its stores.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: UFC champion booted from Frontier flight after being told he couldn't sit in exit row
item.description: Former UFC lightweight champion Khabib Nurmagomedov was removed from a Frontier Airways flight on Saturday after refusing to state whether he would be able to assist other travelers in the event of an emergency, a spokesperson for Frontier told Nexstar on
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump flies US flag at full height before end of 30-day mourning period following Carter's death
item.description: U.S. flags at President-elect Donald Trump 's private Mar-a-Lago club are back to flying at full height.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas man accused of sending Caitlin Clark violent messages on X: court docs
item.description: A Texas man is accused of sending sexually violent messages from his X account to Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark and then driving to Indianapolis, court documents reveal.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, Fox 5

item.title: Oscars push back nominations announcement amid Calif. wildfires
item.description: With fires still active in the Los Angeles area, the film academy also extended the nominations voting period for its members through Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: List: The 'overused' words banished for 2025 by a Michigan university
item.description: Sorry not sorry, but one Michigan university would appreciate if you did not utilize these words, period.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, ABC 4

item.title: Former Disney World employee to plead guilty to hacking menus, changing allergen information: court records
item.description: A former Walt Disney World employee will plead guilty to hacking menu-creation software to include profanity and alter allergy information, court records indicate.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KRON4

item.title: Jayden Daniels leads Commanders to first playoff win in nearly 7,000 days, 23-20 over the Buccaneers
item.description:
New owners. New general manager. New coach. New franchise quarterback.
That’s the formula — with an emphasis on the QB — that Washington rode to playoff success after nearly two decades of futility.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lamar Jackson and Josh Allen will face off for a berth in the AFC championship game
item.description: Lamar Jackson vs. Josh Allen won’t decide the NFL MVP next week. The stakes are higher with a spot in the AFC championship game on the line.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Fires burn Los Angeles schools and destroy outdoor education sanctuaries
item.description: For Irina Contreras, a program manager for Los Angeles County’s Department of Arts and Culture, outdoor education was a refuge for both her and her daughter during the pandemic. Now, much of that refuge has been burned in the raging wildfires around Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: CHEYANNE MUMPHREY and SHARON LURYE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Navy veteran cleared of conspiracy charges in high-profile Jan. 6 case is sentenced to time served
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A retired Navy intelligence officer who was cleared of the most serious charges in his U.S. Capitol attack trial alongside Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes avoided additional time behind bars when he was sentenced on Friday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4
